patches.summary: Treat saved config credentials as satisfying doctor env-var availability.
patches.reason: monday-pp-cli auth set-token stores MONDAY_API_TOKEN in config, so doctor should not keep reporting the env var as missing after auth is configured.
patches.validated_outcome: doctor reports env_vars OK after auth set-token, and whoami/boards list succeed against monday.com.

patches.summary: Enable monday GraphQL resources to hydrate the local SQLite store and make sql query the populated store.
patches.reason: The generated sync surface had an empty default resource list and REST-only fetch path, while this printed CLI's monday surface is hand-written GraphQL. The sql command was also a placeholder that always reported the store as empty.
patches.validated_outcome: sync populated workspaces, boards, users, teams, docs, items, columns, groups, updates, and activity_logs into ~/.local/share/monday-pp-cli/data.db; sql now returns local counts from resources.

patches.summary: authHeader must hold a per-client lock across the OAuth token expiry-check, refresh, and read.
patches.reason: sync shares one *Client across concurrent workers; the generated authHeader read Config.{AccessToken,TokenExpiry,RefreshToken} and refreshAccessToken wrote them via SaveTokens with no lock, so concurrent OAuth refresh raced and could double-refresh, consuming a refresh token a sibling already used. api_key auth never takes the refresh branch and is unaffected.
patches.validated_outcome: go test -race ./internal/client/ ./internal/cli/ passes; the refresh expiry check is re-evaluated under the lock so a waiting goroutine reuses the fresh token instead of refreshing again.

patches.summary: The since command must page through activity_logs per board until each board returns a short page, not fetch page 1 only.
patches.reason: The generated since fired a single page-1 GraphQL call, silently dropping every event past --limit on a busy board and contradicting the 'tail every change since a window' promise. Pagination accumulates per board and stops when no board returns a full page; a runaway page cap is surfaced on stderr rather than truncating silently.
patches.validated_outcome: since accumulates activity across pages for multi-board windows; output shape (board_id, board_name, activity[]) is unchanged and existing cli tests pass.
